data.title: Kraft Heinz (KHC) Stock Falls as Berkshire Weighs Selling Entire 27.5% Stake
data.description:
Shares of Kraft Heinz (KHC) fell 3.8% in after-hours trading yesterday after a regulatory filing revealed that Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.A) (BRK.B) may fully exit its stake. Kraft Heinz filed a prospectus stating that Berkshire could sell its remaining 27.5% stake, or 325,442,152 shares. At January 20's closing price of $23.76, the stake is worth roughly $7.7 billion.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The packaged food giant has been struggling with sluggish sales amid a lack of innovation, underinvestment in brands, fierce competition from healthier alternatives, and rising food prices. The filing does not confirm a sale but enables the selling shareholder to dispose of its holding.

Why Is Berkshire Exiting Its Stake?

Warren Buffett first acquired shares during the 2015 merger between Kraft Foods and H.J. Heinz, alongside Brazilian private equity group 3G Capital. However, the combined company did not progress as expected. Buffett has called the investment a mistake due to excessive optimism about its prospects.

Under Buffett's leadership, Berkshire already wrote down the KHC investment twice and took huge losses: a $3 billion write-down in 2019 and another $3.76 billion in August 2025.

Kraft Heinz Prepares for a Split

Berkshire's potential sale comes ahead of Kraft Heinz's planned split in the second half of this year. In September 2025, KHC announced the breakup, citing the merged company's complex structure, which has hindered effective capital allocation, initiative prioritization, and scaling of promising areas.

The split will create two companies, one for groceries and the other for sauces and spreads, so each can focus resources better and improve results. Kraft Heinz also pointed to shifting consumer tastes toward healthier foods and weak sales, including a 3% drop in 2024, as factors driving the decision.

KHC appointed new CEO Steve Cahillane on January 1, 2026, to oversee the split, the same day when Greg Abel took over Berkshire's helm from Buffett. Both Abel and Buffett have opposed the split, which could also be one of the reasons for Berkshire's potential exit.

Why Is Kraft Heinz Stock So Low?

KHC stock has lost 19.4% over the past year amid ongoing challenges. On TipRanks, Kraft Heinz has a Hold consensus rating based on zero Buys, 13 Holds, and one Sell rating. The average Kraft Heinz price target of $25.38 implies 6.8% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: ImmunityBio Stock (IBRX) Keeps Flying as FDA Update Fuels Momentum
data.description:
Shares of ImmunityBio (IBRX) extended their rally on Tuesday, climbing more than 17% as heavy trading activity continued to fuel momentum. The stock has now surged nearly 200% over the past six sessions, putting it firmly in the spotlight as one of the market's top short-squeeze plays.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

That momentum was driven by new regulatory news. ImmunityBio said the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has asked for additional information on whether its drug ANKTIVA could be approved to treat a broader group of bladder cancer patients. Importantly, the agency did not request new clinical trials. Management said it plans to submit the requested data within 30 days.

The company also drew attention on Monday after it shared a podcast appearance by its founder and executive chairman Patrick Soon-Shiong on The Sean Spicer Show, where he discussed FDA oversight and cancer treatment approvals.

ANKTIVA Is Powering ImmunityBio's Momentum

ANKTIVA is an IL‑15-based therapy designed to activate T cells and natural killer cells. It is quickly becoming a key growth driver for ImmunityBio, as demand for the bladder cancer therapy continues to rise. The company said unit sales jumped 750% in 2025 compared with 2024, reflecting wider use of the treatment.

That surge helped push product revenue sharply higher last year, with ImmunityBio reporting about $113 million in net product revenue for 2025, up from roughly $15 million a year earlier. The company is also making progress in its Phase 2b trial for first-line bladder cancer, with full enrollment expected in the first half of 2026.

While the stock remains volatile, the rapid growth in Anktiva sales is helping support ImmunityBio's recent rally.

Is ImmunityBio a Good Stock to Buy? 

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on IBRX stock based on three Buys assigned in the past three months. Further, the average ImmunityBio price target of $8.00 per share implies 23.46% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: "Agonizingly Slow at First": Tesla Stock Faces Reality Check on Cybercab and Optimus Output
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) investors got a dose of realism on Tuesday after CEO Elon Musk warned that early production of the company's Cybercab robotaxi and its humanoid robot, Optimus, will move at an "agonizingly slow" pace before picking up speed later.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Musk's comments came in response to a post on X suggesting Cybercab production could begin in less than 100 days. While that timeline caught investor attention, Musk quickly tempered expectations, saying the ramp depends heavily on complexity.

"For Cybercab and Optimus, almost everything is new," Musk wrote. "So the early production rate will be agonizingly slow, but eventually end up being insanely fast."

A Big Bet with a Long Runway

The update is important because much of Tesla's roughly $1.39 trillion valuation is based on future bets like self-driving technology and humanoid robots, not on current electric vehicle sales, which still bring in most of the company's revenue and profit.

The Cybercab is a fully autonomous, two-seat vehicle with no steering wheel or pedals, showing how far Tesla is moving beyond traditional cars. The company plans to begin volume production of the Cybercab in 2026, with Optimus production expected to start toward the end of that year.

Early Testing Is Underway, but Scale Will Take Time

Tesla has already taken some early steps. In December, Musk said the company was testing robotaxis without safety monitors in the front passenger seat. Last year, Tesla also launched a limited robotaxi service in Austin, Texas, using Model Y vehicles equipped with its Full Self-Driving software. These early tests were limited to specific areas and still included human oversight.

Even so, scaling production remains a major challenge, and Musk's comments suggest investors should expect a slow build rather than quick results.

Is Tesla Stock Worth Buying?

Analysts remain sidelined on Tesla. On TipRanks, Tesla shares have a Hold consensus rating based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sell ratings. The average Tesla price target of $397.47 implies 5.19% downside potential from current levels. Over the past year, TSLA stock has gained 1.14%.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Elon Musk's xAI Is Testing AI 'Employees,' but the Results Are Sometimes Awkward
data.description:
Elon Musk's AI firm, xAI, is trying to figure out how to automate white-collar workers. In a recent podcast, a former engineer explained that xAI has been testing "human emulators," which are AI systems designed to behave like real employees. These virtual workers are already being used internally and are listed on org charts just like humans. In some cases, real employees did not even realize they were interacting with AI.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Interestingly, the engineer, Sulaiman Ghori, said that these AI workers can sometimes cause confusion due to hallucinations or odd behaviors. For example, an AI might ask a coworker to come talk in person, only for that person to arrive at an empty desk. Notably, Ghori worked on xAI's Macrohard team, which Elon Musk has described as an effort to build AI that can automatically create software. The long-term goal is to challenge traditional enterprise tools from companies like Microsoft (MSFT), but Ghori made it clear that much of the work today is still manual and customized on a case-by-case basis.

In addition, he explained that building virtual employees for customers is difficult because people often forget to describe all the small steps involved in their jobs. Therefore, to improve results, xAI is considering running as many as one million human emulators at once, potentially by using spare computing power from Tesla (TSLA) vehicles while they are charging.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

When it comes to Elon Musk's companies, most of them are privately held. However, retail investors can invest in his most popular company, Tesla. Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $397.47 per share implies 6% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Nvidia (NVDA) Invests $150 Million in AI Firm Baseten
data.description:
Chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) has invested $150 million in Baseten, a start-up company that's focused on providing inference for artificial intelligence (AI) applications.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nvidia's investment was part of a funding round that generated $300 million for Baseten at a $5 billion valuation. The latest funding round was led by IVP, a technology-focused venture capital firm, and CapitalG, which is Google parent company Alphabet's (GOOGL) venture fund.

Baseten last completed a funding raise of $150 million in September 2025. "As model-driven products become ubiquitous, we will be the invisible infrastructure behind the AI-first economy," said Baseten CEO Tuhin Srivastava in a news release.

AI Inference Explained

AI inference is the process where an artificial intelligence model applies what it has learned during training to real-world tasks such as identifying objects, translating languages, and answering questions. The AI model takes the knowledge it has learned and uses it to analyze data and make predictions, decisions, or generate outputs.

Baseten recently announced that it is offering AI companies text-to-video inferencing in the U.S., Finland and France. Nvidia and Baseten already have a relationship. Baseten provides support for Nvidia's Nemotron 3 Nano, a highly accurate, high-efficiency AI model.

Is NVDA Stock a Buy?

Nvidia's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 41 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 39 Buy, one Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the past three months. The average NVDA price target of $263.44 implies 46.91% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Rise After S&P 500 (SPX) and Dow See Steep One-Day Losses
data.description:
U.S. stock futures rose late Tuesday after the market suffered its worst day in three months. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were up 0.32%, 0.21%, and 0.3%, respectively, at 9:00 p.m. EDT on January 20.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In yesterday's trading session, stocks fell sharply as President Donald Trump renewed tariff threats linked to Greenland ahead of his Davos appearance on Wednesday. The Dow dropped about 1.8%, while the S&P 500 slid roughly 2.1%. Technology stocks led the selloff, pushing the Nasdaq Composite down 2.4%. All three major indexes posted their worst daily losses since October 10 and turned negative for 2026.

Trump also threatened 200% tariffs on French wine and Champagne after reports that French President Emmanuel Macron declined a role on Trump's proposed Gaza "Board of Peace."

Investors are now turning their focus to earnings. Netflix (NFLX) shares fell in after-hours trading despite a narrow fourth-quarter earnings beat. Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), Halliburton (HAL), and Travelers (TRV) are set to report results on Wednesday.

data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: "Avoid This Worst Case Scenario," Says Investor About Tesla Stock
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) stock often trades as if the Great Man theory were alive and well – the notion that history is shaped by extraordinary individuals rather than broader societal forces or global trends.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Case in point: TSLA shares have surged 67% since Elon Musk said in late April that he would scale back much of his DOGE work to refocus on Tesla, a shift investors clearly interpreted as a turning point. Shareholders then doubled down on that belief, voting to reinstate a compensation package for Musk that could ultimately approach $1 trillion.

The Great Man worldview could also help explain the so-called Musk premium that investors must pay to own a piece of the Tesla story, as its price-to-earnings ratio – currently hovering around 300x – is dramatically higher than the rest of its peers in the automotive space. While good investments are by nature forward leaning, a purchase of TSLA stock takes that principle to another level.

After all, the promise of Tesla nowadays relies less on its current EV sales or energy business. Instead, it is a leap of faith that the Musk-led company will succeed in delivering trillions of dollars in AI-fueled growth, specifically when it comes to robotaxis and robots.

Investor Stuart Allsopp understands this calculus, but he thinks it has gotten way out of hand.

"While betting against Elon Musk has been a losing bet for a long time, I believe the optimism priced into Tesla's stock is too extreme," explains the investor.

Allsopp points out that Tesla's valuation reflects unbridled optimism about these future industries, which is divorced from the current reality of the company's falling EV fundamentals.

Not only are Tesla's vehicle deliveries falling, but its margins are getting squeezed as its market share shrinks. Allsopp cites the company's deteriorating EPS, which has fallen 62% from a 2023 peak.

Moreover, if the company were to deliver a 10% rate of return on its current market cap of $1.5 trillion (or 2.5% of the entire S&P500), it would need to reach $150 billion in earnings in the next five years. To put this into perspective, when accounting for stock-based compensation, Allsopp calculates that the company made just over $4 billion during the past twelve months.

So, rather than hope for the best, Allsopp prefers to prepare for the worst.

"I firmly believe that when it comes to investing, too little attention is paid to avoiding worst-case scenarios," emphasizes Allsopp.

On that note, the investor is predicting that TSLA's share price will be sinking dramatically going forward.

"The stock has experienced declines of over 50% on five different occasions over the past six years, and another such decline looks increasingly likely," sums up Allsopp, who rates TSLA a Strong Sell. (To watch Stuart Allsopp's track record, click here)

There is scant consensus on Wall Street, with some supporting this view and others vehemently opposed. With 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and 7 Sells, TSLA carries a consensus Hold (i.e., Neutral) rating. Its 12-month average price target of $397.47 would translate into losses of ~5% going forward. (See TSLA stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: "We Need to Bring this Stuff In-House": Tesla Stock (NASDAQ:TSLA) Sinks After Discovering Why FSD Is Not Prized
data.description:
It seemed like a good idea at the time for electric vehicle giant Tesla (TSLA). Develop a program that allows cars to drive themselves, and then license it out to all those other car companies who did not spend all that time and cash to develop their own. The problem, however, is that those potential customers want their own instead. This revelation sent Tesla shares sliding, down nearly 3% in Tuesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ford (F) reached out in a recent report, with its head of electrical engineering Paul Costa spelling out just why licensing Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) program is a bad idea. "To integrate, I can't do this with all these suppliers. We need to bring this stuff in-house, and it allows for this ability to do the trifecta at once: smaller, cheaper, and higher performance."

So while there are significant challenges to "going in-house," these automakers are also able to better customize their own self-driving systems to their market's requirements. Some may want to use a LIDAR-based system. Others might prefer to go with cameras. If they licensed FSD, they would have to do it Tesla's way. Building their own, meanwhile, allows them to play to their own strengths. Plus, developing systems that can confer with each other as required is actually cheaper to do when the whole thing is house-built.

Big in South Korea

Meanwhile, new reports noted that Tesla is an absolute winner in South Korea, being responsible for much of the country's electric vehicle sector growth in 2025. As it turns out, registrations for Teslas were up over 50% in 2025, and electric vehicle penetration overall cleared the 10% mark for the first time in the country.

It turns out that the Model Y, which is built in the Shanghai Gigafactory, is an easy import from Shanghai to South Korea. In fact, the Model Y actually accounts for 26.6% of the pure electric passenger vehicle market, reports note.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 3.17% rally in its share price over the past year, the average TSLA price target of $397.47 per share implies 6.38% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson
